question_text: When two identical resistors are connected in series in a circuit, how does the total resistance compare to a single resistor?

options.label: A
options.text: The total resistance is half the value of one resistor

options.label: B
options.text: The total resistance equals the sum of both resistors

options.label: C
options.text: The total resistance is the same as one resistor

options.label: D
options.text: The total resistance is twice the value of one resistor squared

explanation: In a series circuit, resistors add together directly. If you have two 5Ω resistors in series, the total resistance is 5Ω + 5Ω = 10Ω. The current has to push through both resistors, so the total resistance increases.
